title: Technical-spec constraint envelope
description: The machine-readable hard/soft constraint envelope produced by the technical-spec-architect agent alongside TECHNICAL_SPEC.md. Downstream agents (build-plan, acceptance-spec, a coding/IaC agent) consume the HARD constraints as non-negotiable givens and treat SOFT constraints as overridable defaults. The file holds the bounding decisions only; the design rationale narrative lives in the spec.

version.description: Schema version of this file. Bump only on breaking schema changes.

spec.description: Filename of the companion technical specification (e.g. TECHNICAL_SPEC.md).

constraints.description: The flat list of constraints bounding the solution space.

key.description: Stable, unique, kebab-case identifier for the constraint. MUST be unique across the file. This is the load-bearing identity referenced by downstream build-plan tickets' `constraintRefs`, so a linter can check generated code/IaC against it.

kind.description: HARD = non-negotiable, externally imposed; downstream agents may not violate it. SOFT = strong default, overridable with documented justification (requires an escapeHatch).

category.description: Free-form grouping for scannability and routing (e.g. platform, language, datastore, compliance, security, performance, cost, topology).

statement.description: The constraint itself, stated precisely enough to check against (e.g. 'Python 3.12 for all services', 'p99 interaction latency < 200 ms').

rationale.description: Why this constraint exists — the external force (for hard) or the default's justification (for soft). A hard constraint with no real external force is probably soft.

owner.description: The team or role accountable for this constraint. A constraint nobody owns is unenforceable.

escapeHatch.description: REQUIRED for soft constraints: what a valid override looks like (e.g. 'Document the workload need and the chosen alternative in an ADR'). Forbidden on hard constraints — they have no escape hatch.

tracesTo.description: Optional PR-* product-requirement IDs this constraint is motivated by (the traceability spine). Each must look like a PR-* id; the validator checks the shape and, when a product spec is available, existence.

allOf.description: Soft constraints MUST carry an escape hatch.
Hard constraints MUST NOT carry an escape hatch.
